nav.logoutConfirmBody: You will need to sign in with Twitch again to manage your channel.
nav.requestBot: Get the bot for my channel

home.title: ChatWizardBot
home.subtitle: A Twitch chat bot with moderation and custom commands. Manage settings and view stats for channels below.

about.title: About
about.intro: ChatWizardBot is a Twitch chat bot providing moderation, custom commands, counters, and chat statistics across multiple channels.
about.supportText: For bugs, feature requests, or access issues, contact

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: ChatWizardBot is a Twitch chat bot, and this website is its companion statistics panel for the channels the bot serves. This page explains what data the service stores, why it stores it, and what you can do about it.
privacy.collectIntro: The bot joins only channels whose owners asked for it — it does not monitor Twitch at large. For those channels it stores:
privacy.collectChat: Public chat messages (text, Twitch username and user ID, channel, time) and statistics derived from them: message counts, word and emote frequencies, @mention counts.
privacy.collectMod: Moderation actions (bans, timeouts, deleted messages, warnings) with the acting moderator, the affected user and the time, plus moderator activity statistics.
privacy.collectNames: Username history — the usernames a Twitch account has previously used in tracked channels.
privacy.collectAccountIntro: If you log in to this site with your Twitch account, the site additionally stores:
privacy.collectAccount: Your Twitch ID, login, display name and avatar (received from Twitch when you log in), your login session, and your site preferences (language, profile privacy settings).
privacy.collectProfile: A cache of public Twitch profile data (avatar, chat color, display name) used to render pages; entries unused for 30 days are deleted automatically.
privacy.collectOwnerToken: If you own a connected channel: an encrypted copy of your Twitch refresh token, used in the background to keep that channel's moderator list synced with Twitch. It is deleted automatically if you revoke the site's access on Twitch, and you can ask us to delete it sooner (see "Your rights" below).
privacy.why1: The data exists solely to provide channel statistics: leaderboards, word and emote clouds, chat activity, and moderation analytics for channel owners and their moderators. Channels are added only at the owner's request, and the date of that request is recorded.
privacy.why2: Nothing is sold or shared with third parties. The site has no advertising and no third-party analytics.
privacy.cookies1: The site uses only technical cookies: a session cookie that keeps you logged in with Twitch and a cookie that remembers your language choice. There are no tracking or advertising cookies, which is why the site shows no cookie consent banner.
privacy.visibility1: Aggregated channel statistics (top chatters, word clouds, emote boards) are public.
privacy.visibility2: Raw chat logs and a channel's moderation history are visible only to that channel's owner and moderators.
privacy.visibility3: On your personal profile page, the message-volume chart and the activity calendar are hidden by default until you opt in; in your account settings you can also hide the entire profile from everyone — moderators included.
privacy.rights1: You can control what your profile page shows in your account settings at any time.
privacy.rightsContact: To ask what data is stored about you, or to request its correction or deletion, email
privacy.changes1: If this policy changes, the updated version will be published on this page with a new date.

commands.title: Available Commands
commands.subtitle: Signatures shown are defaults - each channel owner can rename them via that channel's settings page. Pick a channel below to see its actual setup.
commands.viewingChannelPrefix: Showing this channel's actual setup for

category.channelCustom: Custom commands added on this channel

desc.addcommand: Create a custom command, or edit its text if it already exists.
desc.settimer: Auto-post that command's text on an interval (minimum 60s); off disables it.
desc.setpin: Toggle auto-pin for that command's sends.
desc.setannounce: Toggle sending that command as a colored chat announcement instead of a plain message.
desc.customcommands: List all custom command names for the channel.
desc.addcounter: Create a counter starting at 0; add mod to restrict updates to mods.
desc.addexception: Grant a user rights to update mod-restricted counters.
desc.topchatters: Top chatters leaderboard for the period.
desc.countword: How many times a word was said today.
desc.countmsg: Your message count and rank for the period.
desc.countunique: Count of unique chatters for the period.
desc.randomclip: Posts a random clip from the channel's most-viewed clips.
desc.update7tv: Re-sync the channel's 7TV emote set into the tracked-word whitelist.
desc.muteduel: Challenge chat or a user to a dice-roll duel; loser gets timed out.
desc.sovet: Get a random Dota 2 item suggestion. Only responds while the stream's category is Dota 2.

useCase.addcommand: Set up a !discord or !socials command once and let chat trigger it any time.
useCase.delcommand: Retire a command that's no longer relevant.
useCase.settimer: Auto-remind viewers of a !discord link every 10 minutes without anyone typing it.
useCase.setpin: Keep an important announcement pinned whenever it's sent.
useCase.setannounce: Make a scheduled reminder actually stand out in a busy chat.
useCase.customcommands: Let viewers discover what commands a channel has set up.
useCase.triggerCustom: Everyday use of any mod-created command.
useCase.addcounter: Track deaths, wins, or any running tally live during a stream.
useCase.delcounter: Remove a counter once a challenge/event is over.
useCase.counters: Check what counters exist before creating a duplicate.
useCase.counterUpdate: Viewers or mods bump a tally as events happen on stream.
useCase.addexception: Let a trusted regular update a mod-only counter without full mod status.
useCase.remexception: Revoke that trust if it's no longer needed.
useCase.topchatters: Shout out the most active chatters at the end of a stream.
useCase.topsmiles: See which emotes the community actually uses most.
useCase.countword: Settle a chat debate about how often something was said.
useCase.countmsg: Check your own chat activity and rank.
useCase.countunique: Gauge how many different people showed up today.
useCase.botinfo: Mods checking the bot is alive and healthy mid-stream.
useCase.randomclip: Break up a quiet moment with a blast from the channel's past.
useCase.update7tv: Pick up newly added 7TV emotes without waiting for the next auto-sync.
useCase.muteduel: A fun PvP wager between two chatters, loser gets timed out.
useCase.muteaccept: Accepting a challenge someone sent you.
useCase.sovet: A lighthearted random Dota 2 item suggestion for chat.

durak.name: Durak
durak.cardDescription: Classic Russian card game — against the computer or online with people. Don't be the last one holding cards!
durak.modeSelectPcDesc: Classic throw-in Durak against a simple AI.
durak.modeSelectPeopleDesc: Rooms of up to 6 players — with friends and viewers.
durak.leaderboardEmpty: No ratings yet — be the first!
durak.statusYourAttack: Your turn — pick a card to attack with
durak.statusYourThrowIn: Defended! Toss in a card of the same rank, or click "Done"
durak.statusYourDefend: Defend with a higher card of the same suit or a trump
durak.draw: Draw — the deck ran out for both of you!

durakMultiplayer.disconnectedFinal: Couldn't reconnect. Please refresh the page.
durakMultiplayer.errorRoomNotFound: Room not found — it may already be closed.
durakMultiplayer.errorRoomNotJoinable: That room's game has already started.
durakMultiplayer.errorRateLimited: Too many rooms created recently — try again later.
durakMultiplayer.errorNotEnoughPlayers: Need at least 2 players to start.
durakMultiplayer.errorNotHost: Only the host can start the game.
durakMultiplayer.errorRoomNotWatchable: That match can't be watched right now.
durakMultiplayer.errorStickerRateLimited: Sending stickers too fast — slow down.
durakMultiplayer.errorNotInLobby: Can't do that once the game has started.
durakMultiplayer.errorPlayerNotFound: That player is no longer in the room.
durakMultiplayer.ongoingMatchesEmpty: No matches in progress right now.
durakMultiplayer.ruleAllowThrowInsDesc: Other players (not just the attacker) can toss in extra cards matching a rank already on the table. Turn off for classic single-card bouts.
durakMultiplayer.ruleAllowTransfersDesc: Instead of defending, a defender who holds a card of the same rank as the attack can lay it down and pass the whole undefended pile to the next player, who becomes the new defender.
durakMultiplayer.rulesHostOnlyHint: Only the host can change this
durakMultiplayer.chooseActionPrompt: This card can beat the attack or transfer it - what do you want to do?
durakMultiplayer.readyCheckSubtitle: The host started the game — confirm you're still here.
durakMultiplayer.readyCheckWaiting: Waiting for other players to accept…
durakMultiplayer.statusYourAttack: Your turn — pick a card to attack with
durakMultiplayer.statusYourThrowIn: Toss in a card of the same rank, or click "Pass"
durakMultiplayer.statusYourDefend: Defend with a higher card of the same suit or a trump
durakMultiplayer.drawResultTitle: Draw — everyone ran out of cards at once!
durakMultiplayer.kickedToast: The host removed you from the room.
durakMultiplayer.mpRule1: Any player except the defender can toss in cards matching a rank already on the table
durakMultiplayer.mpRule2: Defended everything on the table — you become the attacker
durakMultiplayer.mpRule3: Took the cards — the same attacker goes again
durakMultiplayer.mpRule4: Last one left holding cards is the durak
durakMultiplayer.mpRule5: Up to 6 players at one table
durakMultiplayer.mpRule6: Ratings use an Elo system - beating a stronger opponent earns more than beating a weaker one, and losing to a weaker opponent costs more than losing to a stronger one. Your rating appears after your first finished game
durakMultiplayer.mpRule7: Each player has 5 minutes for the whole game - the clock only runs while it's your move. Run out of time and you're out
durakMultiplayer.mpRule8: The host can turn throw-ins off before starting for a classic single-card-per-bout game
durakMultiplayer.mpRule9: Starting a game opens a 45s ready check - everyone (host included) must click Accept, or they're dropped from the room

statistics.modStatsHint: Click a column to sort. Hover a moderator to see their skill pentagon.

channelSettings.subtitle: Changes take effect within a few seconds.
channelSettings.bannedWordsIntro: A user is timed out if they type one of these words and ping the bot with "@ChatWizardBot" in the same message.
channelSettings.timeoutReasonHint: Shown to the user as the reason for their timeout.
channelSettings.spamSignaturesIntro: A message is re-obfuscated (homoglyphs, spacing tricks, etc. normalized back to plain text) before being checked against these signatures. Each signature can have its own ban duration (up to permanent) and its own reason.
channelSettings.spamSignaturesExample: Example signature "subscribe to my channel ExampleChannel" also catches messages using modified/obfuscated fonts to spell it out.
channelSettings.spamBanReasonHint: Used by default for signatures that don't have their own reason.
channelSettings.spamReasonHint: If not set, the shared reason above is used.

errors.duration_invalid: Pick a duration from the list

channelSettings.detectionToggleHint: When on, the bot times out users who type a banned word while pinging it.
channelSettings.sevenTvNotLinked: No 7TV account found for this channel
channelSettings.autoBehaviorIntro: Features the bot performs on its own, without a chat command — each can be turned on or off individually.

groupIntro.chatStats: Commands that report chat activity and emote usage.
groupIntro.customCommands: Chat commands for managing this channel's own custom commands.
groupIntro.counters: Everything related to # counters and who may update them.
groupIntro.other: Commands that don't fit the groups above.

commandDesc.topchatters: Leaderboard of the most active chatters for a time period.
commandDesc.topsmiles: Leaderboard of the most-used emotes for a time period.
commandDesc.countword: Reports how many times a specific word was said.
commandDesc.countmsg: Reports a user's own message count and rank.
commandDesc.countunique: Reports how many unique chatters have spoken.
commandDesc.randomclip: Posts a random clip from the channel's most-viewed clips.
commandDesc.addcommand: Lets mods create and edit custom commands from chat.
commandDesc.delcommand: Lets mods delete a custom command from chat.
commandDesc.settimer: Lets mods set a custom command to auto-post on an interval.
commandDesc.setpin: Lets mods toggle auto-pinning a custom command's sends.
commandDesc.setannounce: Lets mods toggle sending a custom command as a colored chat announcement.
commandDesc.addcounter: Lets mods create a # counter from chat.
commandDesc.delcounter: Lets mods delete a # counter from chat.
commandDesc.exception: Lets mods grant or revoke a user's right to update mod-restricted counters.
commandDesc.update7tv: Lets mods manually re-sync the channel's 7TV emote set.
commandDesc.muteduel: The mini-game where chat can challenge each other to a dice-roll duel.
commandDesc.question: The bot answers yes/no questions when pinged.
commandDesc.directmsg: The bot responds to pings that aren't a recognized command.

channelSettings.autoPostPacingLabel: Auto-post pacing (minimum chat messages between auto-posts)
channelSettings.autoPostPacingHint: How many ordinary chat messages must pass before the next custom command is auto-posted, so auto-sends don't land back-to-back.
channelSettings.counterCooldownHint: Minimum time between two # counter updates in chat.
channelSettings.busyResponsesHint: A random one of these is sent when the bot is pinged but has nothing more specific to say.
channelSettings.yesNoResponsesHint: Sent when someone asks the bot a yes/no question.
channelSettings.autosaveError: Failed to save — check the values or reload the page

settingsChangeLog.title: Change log
settingsChangeLog.intro: Every settings, command, and counter change made here, newest first.
settingsChangeLog.deleteAllConfirm: Delete the entire settings change log across all channels? This can't be undone.

moderatorPermissions.title: Moderator permissions
moderatorPermissions.intro: Choose which moderators may edit this channel's bot settings, custom commands, and counters. Everyone can still view them regardless of this setting.
moderatorPermissions.empty: No moderators registered for this channel yet.

accountSettings.title: Settings
accountSettings.subtitle: Personal preferences for your account on this site.
accountSettings.privacyHint: These settings apply to your profile page on every channel of this site.
accountSettings.privacyHideProfileHint: Hides your entire profile page from everyone, including channel owners and moderators.

errors.accessDeniedMessage: You don't have permission to view this page.
errors.notFoundMessage: The page or channel you're looking for doesn't exist.
errors.serverErrorMessage: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

userDashboard.noMessages: No messages in this channel yet
userDashboard.noTrackedEmotes: This channel has no tracked 7TV emotes
userDashboard.sampled: Showing the most recent messages in this period, not all of them
userDashboard.profileHiddenBody: The user has chosen to keep their chat statistics private.
userDashboard.profileHiddenOwnerHint: You can turn this off in your settings

channelDashboard.noTrackedEmotes: No tracked emotes yet — this channel has no 7TV set synced
channelDashboard.cloudCapNote: Emotes are excluded — they have their own board

customCommands.title: Custom commands
customCommands.subtitle: Created here or with !addcommand in chat — same commands, same bot
customCommands.name: Command
customCommands.responseHint: Used when the stream's current category doesn't match any of the categories below.
customCommands.pin: Pin to chat when posted (moderators only)
customCommands.announce: Send as a chat announcement (highlighted, colored; only moderators will be able to trigger the command)
customCommands.countersHint: Use #counterName to insert a counter's current value
customCommands.categoryOverridesHint: Optional: different text for different categories (e.g. "Dota 2"). The name must exactly match the stream's Twitch category. Blank rows are ignored.
customCommands.saved: Saved — live in chat within a few seconds

errors.name_invalid: Only letters and digits, no spaces or symbols
errors.result_too_long: Response is too long for a Twitch message
errors.timer_invalid: Auto-post interval must be a number of seconds
errors.timer_too_short: Auto-post interval is too short (minimum 60 seconds)
errors.timer_and_pin: A command can have an auto-post timer or auto-pin, not both — Twitch allows only one pinned message per channel
errors.announce_and_pin: A command can be an announcement or auto-pinned, not both — an announcement has no message to pin
errors.category_required: Enter a category for this row, or clear both cells
errors.category_result_required: Enter text for this category, or clear both cells
errors.category_result_too_long: Text is too long for a Twitch message
errors.category_duplicate: Each category can only be listed once

counters.title: Counters
counters.subtitle: Created here or with !addcounter in chat — same counters, same bot
counters.name: Counter
counters.hint: Chat updates it with #name or #name + N; use #name inside a custom command's text to show its value
counters.saved: Saved — live in chat within a few seconds

errors.name_invalid: Only lowercase letters and digits, no spaces or symbols
errors.count_invalid: Value must be a whole number

requestBot.title: Request the bot
requestBot.subtitle: Ask for ChatWizardBot to be added to your Twitch channel.
requestBot.connectedNotice: Your channel is already connected to the bot.
requestBot.pendingBody: An administrator will review it soon. Once approved, the bot joins your channel after its next restart.
requestBot.modNoticeBody: The bot needs moderator rights on your channel to work properly (timeouts, spam protection, deleting messages). Type this in your channel's chat:
requestBot.rejectedRetryHint: You can submit a new request below.
requestBot.messageHint: Anything the administrator should know about your channel.
requestBot.modAckLabel: I have granted (or will grant) the bot moderator rights on my channel

errors.mod_ack_required: Please confirm the moderator-rights checkbox first.

admin.title: Admin panel
admin.subtitle: Bot-join requests, channels, and service health.
admin.visitsIntro: Page views per day for the last 30 days (excludes static files and pages' own background JSON polling).
admin.gamesIntro: Completed sessions/matches per game, plus how many distinct players have saved a score or rating - ranked by sessions. Vs-computer Durak is never counted here - its results never leave the browser.

flash.approved: Request approved and channel registered. The bot joins the channel within a minute.
flash.enabled: Channel enabled. The bot joins it within a minute.
flash.disabled: Channel disabled. The bot leaves it at its next restart.
flash.homepageShown: Channel is now shown on the home page.
flash.homepageHidden: Channel hidden from the home page.
flash.deleted: The entire settings change log has been deleted.

admin.healthNoTokenHint: Scheduled moderator sync is off for these channels until their owner logs in to the site:
admin.requestsApproveHint: Approving registers the channel; the bot joins it after its next restart.
admin.channelsHint: Disabling removes the channel from the site's home page and from the bot's join list at its next restart. "On home page" separately hides a channel from the home page only - the site and bot keep working with it as normal.
